steps.title: Link timing change to swing
steps.content: The researchers changed timing by delaying downbeats and synchronizing offbeats. Jazz musicians were then much more likely to judge the modified versions as having swing.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A credits offbeats alone even though both timing features changed. Choice B discusses whether musicians can manipulate properties, which is not tested. Choice C adds an unsupported comparison between piano and non-piano music. The result points to beat timing as crucial. The answer is D.
